title: Set scrub step to 15 seconds and cancel A-B on invalidating seeks
summary: 0.6.42 raises the default skip or scrub step to 15 seconds and makes explicit slider seeks outside the active A-B range clear A-B instead of silently remapping the seek through the loop window.
changes: The default skip or scrub step now uses 15 seconds instead of 10 seconds.
Explicit normalized or seconds-based seeks now target the real media timeline first, and if that target lands outside the active A-B range the runtime clears and disables A-B before seeking there.
Active A-B still constrains seeks that stay inside the valid range.
reasoning: The current lane wants a firmer transport rule: explicit manual slider movement should not silently reinterpret the operator's target through the A-B window.
A-B is still useful as an active loop, but any seek that invalidates that loop should cancel it cleanly instead of layering more remapping behavior on top.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: Skip or scrub buttons now move in 15-second steps by default.
Clicking or dragging the slider outside the active A-B range should clear A-B and move to the requested point instead of staying trapped inside the loop.
knownIssues: This slice does not add the stateful A-B checkbox yet.
This slice does not yet make image playlists use the slider as an index transport.
